reason: Adjective 'fast' is one core sense — firmly in place / not moving / not loose — which English renders as fixed/firm/solid/stuck/caught by context. Lead the card with the core 'fixed, firm' instead of the secondary 'caught' shade (was 'caught, captured', whose 'captured' wording made it read as a distinct sense). 'caught, stuck' and 'solid (not liquid)' become reference sub-defs. Deliberately NOT split — one word, one sense (teach-simple-users). Its duplicate twin 1152 was dropped in 90-manual/10-dedup.json.
